result.stdout:
dotvvm:
  DotVVM Command-Line Interface

Usage:
  dotvvm [options] [command]

Options:
  -v, --verbose     Print more verbose output
  --version         Show version information
  -?, -h, --help    Show help and usage information

Commands:
  info <target>    Prints metadata about the DotVVM project
  lint <target>    Look for compiler errors in Views and Markup Controls
  add <target>     Add a DotVVM-related thingy
  api <target>     Manage REST API clients

commands.payload:
dotvvm:
  DotVVM Command-Line Interface

Usage:
  dotvvm [options] [command]

Options:
  -v, --verbose     Print more verbose output
  --version         Show version information
  -?, -h, --help    Show help and usage information

Commands:
  info <target>    Prints metadata about the DotVVM project
  lint <target>    Look for compiler errors in Views and Markup Controls
  add <target>     Add a DotVVM-related thingy
  api <target>     Manage REST API clients

result.stdout:
add:
  Add a DotVVM-related thingy

Usage:
  dotvvm add [options] [<target>] [command]

Arguments:
  <target>    Path to a DotVVM project

Options:
  -v, --verbose     Print more verbose output
  -?, -h, --help    Show help and usage information

Commands:
  page <name>         Add a page
  master <name>       Add a master page
  viewmodel <name>    Add a ViewModel
  control <name>      Add a control

commands.payload:
add:
  Add a DotVVM-related thingy

Usage:
  dotvvm add [options] [<target>] [command]

Arguments:
  <target>    Path to a DotVVM project

Options:
  -v, --verbose     Print more verbose output
  -?, -h, --help    Show help and usage information

Commands:
  page <name>         Add a page
  master <name>       Add a master page
  viewmodel <name>    Add a ViewModel
  control <name>      Add a control

result.stdout:
control:
  Add a control

Usage:
  dotvvm add [<target>] control [options] [<name>]

Arguments:
  <target>    Path to a DotVVM project
  <name>      The name of the new thingy

Options:
  -d, --directory <directory>    The directory where the new control is to be placed [default: Controls/]
  -c, --code-behind              Create a C# code-behind class for the control
  -v, --verbose                  Print more verbose output
  -?, -h, --help                 Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
master:
  Add a master page

Usage:
  dotvvm add [<target>] master [options] [<name>]

Arguments:
  <target>    Path to a DotVVM project
  <name>      The name of the new thingy

Options:
  -m, --master <master>          The @master page of the new page
  -d, --directory <directory>    The directory where the new page is to be placed [default: Views/]
  -v, --verbose                  Print more verbose output
  -?, -h, --help                 Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
api:
  Manage REST API clients

Usage:
  dotvvm api [options] [<target>] [command]

Arguments:
  <target>    Path to a DotVVM project

Options:
  -v, --verbose     Print more verbose output
  -?, -h, --help    Show help and usage information

Commands:
  create <definition>    Create a REST API client
  regen <definition>     Regenerate a specific API client or all of them

commands.payload:
api:
  Manage REST API clients

Usage:
  dotvvm api [options] [<target>] [command]

Arguments:
  <target>    Path to a DotVVM project

Options:
  -v, --verbose     Print more verbose output
  -?, -h, --help    Show help and usage information

Commands:
  create <definition>    Create a REST API client
  regen <definition>     Regenerate a specific API client or all of them

result.stdout:
create:
  Create a REST API client

Usage:
  dotvvm api [<target>] create [options] <definition>

Arguments:
  <target>        Path to a DotVVM project
  <definition>    Path or a URL to an OpenAPI definition

Options:
  --namespace <namespace>    The namespace of the generated C# API client
  --cs-path <cs-path>        Path to the generated C# client
  --ts-path <ts-path>        Path to the generated TypeScript client
  -v, --verbose              Print more verbose output
  -?, -h, --help             Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
create:
  Create a REST API client

Usage:
  dotvvm api [<target>] create [options] <definition>

Arguments:
  <target>        Path to a DotVVM project
  <definition>    Path or a URL to an OpenAPI definition

Options:
  --namespace <namespace>    The namespace of the generated C# API client
  --cs-path <cs-path>        Path to the generated C# client
  --ts-path <ts-path>        Path to the generated TypeScript client
  -v, --verbose              Print more verbose output
  -?, -h, --help             Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
regen:
  Regenerate a specific API client or all of them

Usage:
  dotvvm api [<target>] regen [options] [<definition>]

Arguments:
  <target>        Path to a DotVVM project
  <definition>    Path or a URL to an OpenAPI file

Options:
  --config <config>    Path to the DotVVM API configuration JSON
  -v, --verbose        Print more verbose output
  -?, -h, --help       Show help and usage information

commands.payload:

result.stdout:
lint:
  Look for compiler errors in Views and Markup Controls

Usage:
  dotvvm lint [options] [<target>]

Arguments:
  <target>    Path to a DotVVM project

Options:
  --no-build                         Don't build the MSBuild project.
  --configuration <configuration>    The configuration used to build the project. [default: Debug]
  --framework <framework>            The target framework used to build the project.
  -v, --verbose                      Print more verbose output
  -?, -h, --help                     Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
lint:
  Look for compiler errors in Views and Markup Controls

Usage:
  dotvvm lint [options] [<target>]

Arguments:
  <target>    Path to a DotVVM project

Options:
  --no-build                         Don't build the MSBuild project.
  --configuration <configuration>    The configuration used to build the project. [default: Debug]
  --framework <framework>            The target framework used to build the project.
  -v, --verbose                      Print more verbose output
  -?, -h, --help                     Show help and usage information
